purpose: Inventory of official student visa, study permit, and related educational migration data sources, with source-level schemas and comparability notes.
project_scope_note: This file documents public data sources about visa or permit requests and decisions after study admission and before study start. It is not about university rankings or admissions.

generated_artifact_applicant_scope.policy: Exclude explicitly identifiable dependant applicants from generated common tables and SQLite.
generated_artifact_applicant_scope.Australia: Keep Applicant Type = Primary; preserve Secondary only in source-level extracted CSV files.
generated_artifact_applicant_scope.United Kingdom: Keep Applicant type = Main Applicant in entry-clearance data; sponsored-study course-level data are already main-applicant-only. Exclude All and Dependant entry-clearance rows from generated common artifacts.
generated_artifact_applicant_scope.United States: Keep F1 and M1 primary student classes; exclude F2 and M2 dependent classes.
generated_artifact_applicant_scope.other_sources: When a source does not expose a separable applicant-type dimension, retain the published rows and document that primary-only coverage cannot be guaranteed.

un_m49.title: Standard country or area codes for statistical use (M49)
un_m49.release_date: Not stated; UNSD maintains this as a live standard
un_m49.preparation_note: The official semicolon-delimited CSV is used directly as the canonical country and area reference.
un_m49.normalization_note: Canonical countries/areas, reviewed aliases, aggregates, unknown labels, and unmatched labels are represented separately. Territories with their own UNSD record are not assigned to an administering sovereign country.

countries.overall_description: Annual HTML tables of offshore or overseas student visa application decisions by country, territory, or special administrative region. These are currently the cleanest official source for origin-country-level student visa applications, approvals, declines, and rates.

source_records.title: Offshore student visa application decisions for 2022

columns.name: Country/Territory/Special administrative region
columns.description: Country, territory, or special administrative region of the applicant.

columns.name: Number approved
columns.description: Number of completed applications approved during the calendar year.

columns.name: Number declined
columns.description: Number of completed applications declined during the calendar year.

columns.name: Decline rate
columns.description: Reported decline percentage.

useful_notes: The 2022 page does not expose the same columns as later years: there is no explicit application volume or approval-rate column in the visible table.
Application volume may be derivable as approved plus declined only if withdrawals or other outcomes are not present; do not assume this without checking source definitions.

source_records.title: Offshore student visa application decisions for 2023

coverage.excluded_rows: Countries where no applications were completed in 2023 are excluded.

columns.name: Country/Territory/Special administrative region
columns.description: Country, territory, or special administrative region of the applicant.

columns.name: Volume of visa applications
columns.description: Number of visa applications decided and completed during the calendar year.

columns.name: Number approved
columns.description: Number of applications approved.

columns.name: Approval rate
columns.description: Reported approval percentage.

columns.name: Number declined
columns.description: Number of applications declined.

columns.name: Decline rate
columns.description: Reported decline percentage.

useful_notes: The table is already close to the project target schema: origin, destination, year, applications, approvals, refusals, approval rate.
Rates may not reconcile perfectly with counts because of rounding and possible non-approval/non-decline outcomes.

source_records.title: Overseas student visa application decisions for 2024

coverage.excluded_rows: Countries where no applications were completed in 2024 are excluded.

columns.name: Country/Territory/Special administrative region
columns.description: Country, territory, or special administrative region of the applicant.

columns.name: Volume of visa applications
columns.description: Number of visa applications decided and completed during the calendar year.

columns.name: Number approved
columns.description: Number of applications approved.

columns.name: Approval rate
columns.description: Reported approval percentage.

columns.name: Number declined
columns.description: Number of applications declined.

columns.name: Decline rate
columns.description: Reported decline percentage.

useful_notes: The source states that approval and decline percentages may not add up to 100 percent because withdrawals are not included.
Keep withdrawals as an implicit missing outcome unless a later source exposes them directly.

source_records.title: Overseas student visa application decisions for 2025

coverage.excluded_rows: Countries where no applications were completed in 2025 are excluded.

columns.name: Country/Territory/Special administrative region
columns.description: Country, territory, or special administrative region of the applicant.

columns.name: Volume of visa applications
columns.description: Number of visa applications decided and completed during the calendar year as of the source snapshot date.

columns.name: Number approved
columns.description: Number of applications approved.

columns.name: Approval rate
columns.description: Reported approval percentage.

columns.name: Number declined
columns.description: Number of applications declined.

columns.name: Decline rate
columns.description: Reported decline percentage.

useful_notes: The 2025 source labels the table as calendar year 2025 as of 16 February 2026.
The included visa-type list is slightly broader than the 2022-2024 descriptions because it includes Foreign Government Supported Student Visa explicitly.

comparability_notes: Use New Zealand as a reference-quality source for country-level applications, approvals, declines, and rates.
The 2022 schema differs from 2023-2025 and should remain a separate source record.
The data is offshore or overseas only and should not be mixed with onshore student visa decisions without a separate dimension.

countries.publisher: Australian Government Department of Home Affairs
countries.overall_description: The Student visa program dataset on data.gov.au publishes monthly XLSX pivot workbooks and a small CSV resource index for student visa lodgements, grants, and grant rates. It covers subclass 500 and subclasses 570 to 576 in current and previous financial years.
countries.reference_period: 2005-07-01 to 2026-04-30 in the downloaded pivot caches and official package metadata

source_records.title: BP0015 Student visas lodged report at 2026-04-30
source_records.local_filename_note: The preserved local filename says 2026-02-28, but the downloaded bytes are the 2026-04-30 resource: the pivot cache runs through 30 April 2026 and the saved CKAN package metadata identifies the resource as 2026-04-30.

columns.name: Financial Year of Visa Lodged
columns.description: Financial year in which the visa application was lodged.

columns.name: Financial Year Quarter
columns.description: Financial year quarter used in the BP visa statistics reports.

columns.name: Month
columns.description: Month as recorded in the statistics.

columns.name: Client Location
columns.description: Location of the client at the time of lodging an application unless specified otherwise. Usually onshore/offshore, but may be country-level for applications processed in some systems.

columns.name: Lodgement Channel
columns.description: Channel used when lodging the visa application.

columns.name: Sector
columns.description: Education sector for the student visa application.

columns.name: Applicant Type
columns.description: Applicant type such as primary applicant or dependant, depending on workbook values.

columns.name: Education Provider Registered State
columns.description: Registered state of the education provider shown on the confirmation of enrolment.

columns.name: Gender
columns.description: Gender recorded in the visa application.

columns.name: Citizenship Country
columns.description: Country of citizenship of the visa applicant or visa holder. If more than one citizenship exists, the travel-document citizenship or nominated citizenship is used.

columns.name: Age Group
columns.description: Age grouping for the applicant cohort.

columns.name: Total
columns.description: Number of student visa applications lodged.

source_records.local_filename_note: The preserved local filename says 2026-02-28, but the downloaded bytes are the 2026-04-30 resource according to the pivot-cache period and saved CKAN package metadata.

columns.name: Lodgement Channel

columns.name: Sector

columns.name: Applicant Type
columns.description: Applicant type such as primary applicant or dependant, depending on workbook values.

columns.name: Education Provider Registered State
columns.description: Registered state of the education provider shown on the confirmation of enrolment.

columns.name: Gender

source_records.title: BP0015 Student visa grant rates at 2026-04-30
source_records.local_filename_note: The preserved local filename says 2026-02-28, but the downloaded bytes are the 2026-04-30 resource according to the pivot-cache period and saved CKAN package metadata.

columns.name: Financial Year of Decision
columns.description: Financial year in which the decision was recorded.

columns.name: Financial Year Quarter
columns.description: Financial year quarter used in the BP visa statistics reports.

columns.name: Month
columns.description: Month as recorded in the statistics.

columns.name: Client Location
columns.description: Location of the client at the time of lodging an application unless specified otherwise.

columns.name: Lodgement Channel
columns.description: Channel used when lodging the visa application.

columns.name: Sector
columns.description: Education sector for the student visa application.

columns.name: Applicant Type
columns.description: Applicant type such as primary applicant or dependant, depending on workbook values.

columns.name: Education Provider Registered State
columns.description: Registered state of the education provider shown on the confirmation of enrolment.

columns.name: Gender
columns.description: Gender recorded in the visa application.

columns.name: Citizenship Country
columns.description: Country of citizenship of the visa applicant or visa holder.

columns.name: Age Group
columns.description: Age grouping for the applicant cohort.

columns.name: Grant Total
columns.description: Number of grants used in the grant-rate calculation.

columns.name: Refused Total
columns.description: Number of refusals used in the grant-rate calculation.

columns.name: Total
columns.description: Decision total used in the grant-rate calculation.

columns.name: Grant Rate
columns.description: Number of visas granted divided by the sum of visas granted and refused decisions.

comparability_notes: The Australian source distinguishes lodgement, grant, and decision-based grant rate workbooks. These should not be treated as one table without explicit joining keys.
Grant rate is decision-based: grants divided by grants plus refusals, not grants divided by lodged applications.
The downloaded workbooks are pivot-style XLSX files. Extraction should use pivot cache fields or a spreadsheet engine rather than only visible pivot cells.

The common schema retains Lodgement Channel for all three workbooks and Last Visa Held for the grants workbook. Omitting those dimensions caused repeated visible keys with different values; the source records are unique when all pivot-cache dimensions are retained.
For every grant-rate pivot record, Grant Total plus Refused Total equals Total. The validation script checks this reconciliation invariant.
Source-level extraction preserves Primary and Secondary applicant types, but generated common tables and SQLite retain Primary applicants only.

countries.overall_description: GOV.UK Immigration system statistics publish detailed Excel datasets for entry clearance visa applications and outcomes, plus a course-level education visa workbook derived from matching entry clearance visa records to Confirmation of Acceptance for Studies records.

source_records.title: Entry clearance visa applications and outcomes detailed datasets, year ending March 2026
source_records.format: XLSX workbook with pivot sheets and dataset sheets

Data from Q1 2025 onwards are provisional.

columns.name: Year

useful_notes: This workbook relates to main applicants applying outside the UK.
It is derived by matching entry clearance visa records and Confirmation of Acceptance for Studies records.
A small proportion of applications and grants not matched to a CAS are excluded.
Data from Q1 2025 onwards are provisional.

comparability_notes: The entry-clearance workbook can support study visa applications and outcomes by nationality, but it is broader than student visas and requires filtering.
The course-level workbook is useful for education-level analysis but excludes unmatched CAS records and is main-applicant-only.
Applications and decisions occur in different periods, so a same-quarter approval rate needs careful definition.
Source-level entry-clearance extraction preserves All, Main Applicant, and Dependant rows; generated common tables and SQLite retain Main Applicant only.

countries.overall_description: Canada publishes substantial study permit holder and student arrival data through IRCC/Open Canada, and also publishes some application approval/refusal outcome tables through official parliamentary and committee transparency pages. The approval-rate sources are usually by country of residence, while the permit-holder volume sources are usually by country of citizenship.

source_records.title: CIMM - Student Approval Rates by Country of Residence - March 24, 2022
source_records.description: Number of study permit applications excluding extensions processed by IRCC, broken down by final decision year and country of residence. This is the strongest currently retrieved Canada source for all-country study permit approval-rate modelling.

columns.name: Country of Residence
columns.description: Applicant country of residence, not citizenship.

columns.name: Approved
columns.description: Number of processed study permit applications approved.

columns.name: Approval rate
columns.description: Reported approval percentage.

columns.name: Refused
columns.description: Number of processed study permit applications refused.

columns.name: Total
columns.description: Total processed applications in the period.

useful_notes: The page title says country of residence and the table is explicitly for study permit applications excluding extensions.
The extracted CSV adds year, period label, period type, and a blank withdrawn column so it can align with later Canada outcome tables.
Use this source for strict Canada approval-rate analysis only with origin_country_type = country_of_residence.

source_records.title: CIMM - Student Approval Rates by Country of Residence - May 12, 2022
source_records.description: Official CIMM page describing study permit approval rates by country of residence through March 2022. The downloaded page contains summary/truncated tables rather than all countries, so it is retained as provenance but is not currently extracted as a full country-level CSV.

columns.name: Country of Residence
columns.description: Applicant country of residence.

columns.name: Approved
columns.description: Number of processed study permit applications approved.

columns.name: Refused
columns.description: Number of processed study permit applications refused.

columns.name: Total
columns.description: Total processed applications in the period.

columns.name: Approval Rate
columns.description: Reported approval percentage.

useful_notes: The page states data source COGNOS (MBR), extracted as of April 20, 2022.
Because the HTML contains ellipses/truncated country rows, use the March 24, 2022 page for full extraction through January 2022.

source_records.period_covered: 2020-2024 depending on table; main intake/outcome tables cover 2023 and 2024 through September 30

source_records.format: Open Canada dataset with multiple XLSX, XLS, and CSV resources
source_records.description: Temporary residents who are in Canada on a study permit in the observed calendar year. Datasets include study permit holders by year in which permits became effective, with a valid permit in a calendar year, or with a valid permit on December 31.

useful_notes: Values between 0 and 5 are suppressed as --, and other values are rounded to the nearest multiple of 5.
Rounded values may not sum to totals.
This dataset is useful for study permit volumes and flows, but not directly for application refusal or approval rates.

columns.name: Month

columns.description: Number of people issued study permits in that month for arrivals, or number of active study permit holders for stock tables depending on section.

useful_notes: The page counts arrivals based on the number of people issued study or work permits in the month.
If someone was issued both a study and work permit in the same month, they are counted under the study permit group.
Student arrivals exclude asylum claimants and permit extensions.
Values between 0 and 5 are suppressed as -- and other values are rounded to the closest multiple of 5.

source_records.title: OLLO Order Paper Question Q-2700 - French-speaking and bilingual study permit applications processed by country of residence

columns.name: Country of Residence
columns.description: Declared country of residence of the applicant.

columns.name: Approved
columns.description: Number of study permit applications approved in the period.

columns.name: Approval Rate
columns.description: Source-reported approval rate.

columns.name: Refused
columns.description: Number of study permit applications refused in the period.

columns.name: Total Processed
columns.description: Total processed across 2023 and 2024 January-April in the source table.

useful_notes: This is not all Canada study permit applicants; it is limited to French-speaking or bilingual applicants.
The extracted rows were inserted into SQLite only and intentionally not added to data/student_visa_common_long.csv.
The source states that extensions are excluded.

source_records.title: CIMM Question No. Q-78 - French-speaking international students summary
useful_notes: The HTML page references Annex A but the downloaded English page does not expose the full country table; it was preserved as provenance but not converted to CSV in this run.

source_records.title: COW International Students - June 9, 2025
useful_notes: This page provides aggregate context, including 2024 approved new study permits and 2025 intake trend language, but no country-level table was extracted in this run.

comparability_notes: Canada can support approval-rate analysis for 2019, 2020, 2021, and January 2022 using the CIMM all-country country-of-residence source.
Canada can support more recent 2022-2024 outcome analysis only for top 10 country-of-residence rows plus Other Countries and Grand Total from the retrieved CIMM snapshots.

The approval/outcome sources use country of residence, while the IRCC/Open Canada permit-holder sources use country of citizenship. Do not merge these origin concepts without an explicit origin_country_type field.
Do not compare Canada's issued permit or permit-holder counts directly to New Zealand or Australia application approval rates.
The OLLO Q-2700 rows are a French-speaking/bilingual applicant subset and must not be merged with all-applicant Canada approval-rate rows without a subset flag.
The Q-2700 extracted rows were added to SQLite only; data/student_visa_common_long.csv remains the broader common CSV build output.

countries.publisher: U.S. Department of State, Bureau of Consular Affairs
countries.overall_description: Official nonimmigrant visa statistics. The current extracted dataset uses the multi-year NIV Detail Table for F and M student visa classes by nationality and fiscal year. This is an issuance dataset, not an application/refusal or approval-rate dataset by nationality.

source_records.title: FY1997-2024 NIV Detail Table

columns.name: Fiscal Year sheet
columns.description: Each worksheet represents one U.S. fiscal year.

columns.name: Nationality
columns.description: Nationality as reported in the Department of State detail table.

columns.name: F1
columns.description: Academic student visa issuances.

columns.name: F2
columns.description: Dependent of F1 student visa issuances.

columns.name: M1
columns.description: Vocational/nonacademic student visa issuances.

columns.name: M2
columns.description: Dependent of M1 student visa issuances.

useful_notes: The table reports issuances, not applications received, refusals, or approval rates by nationality.
Region separator rows and grand-total rows are excluded during extraction.
F1 and M1 are primary student classes; F2 and M2 are dependent classes.

source_records.title: NIV Workload by Visa Category

columns.name: Visa category
columns.description: Nonimmigrant visa category/class grouping.

columns.name: Applications
columns.description: Worldwide workload count by visa category.

columns.name: Issuances
columns.description: Worldwide issuance count by visa category.

columns.name: Refusals
columns.description: Worldwide refusal count by visa category.

useful_notes: These PDFs may support worldwide visa-class approval/refusal rates, but not nationality-level approval rates.
They were downloaded for provenance but not parsed into SQLite in the current extraction.

comparability_notes: The United States rows currently added to SQLite are issuance counts only. They cannot support nationality-level approval-rate modelling without a separate official application/refusal denominator by nationality.
Do not compare US issuances directly to Australia/New Zealand grant rates or Canada outcome rates as an approval probability.
Generated source-level and common CSVs and SQLite retain F1 and M1 only. F2 and M2 dependent classes remain documented as fields in the raw workbook but are excluded from generated artifacts.

cross_country_notes: New Zealand and Australia are the strongest first comparative pair because both expose origin-country student visa decision rates.
Australia grant rates are decision-based, while New Zealand approval rates are reported against the source's application volume. Definitions must be aligned before comparison.

The UK can likely support student applications and outcomes by nationality after filtering entry clearance data to study categories, but applications and decisions may not refer to the same cohort or quarter.

Canada has official approval/outcome data through CIMM transparency pages, but it is by country of residence and has limited all-country coverage after January 2022; the Open Canada files remain permit volume sources, not approval-rate sources.
United States public nationality-level data currently supports student visa issuances by F/M class, not approval rates.
